description: Fix get_file_content to not hardcode 'main' as the default branch, allowing repos with other default branches (master, develop, trunk) to work.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/bitbucket && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Remove hardcoded 'main' fallback for ref parameter
userStories.description: The get_file_content tool defaults ref to 'main' when not specified. Bitbucket's API uses the repo's default branch when no ref is provided, so omitting the ref is the correct behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: When ref is not provided, the API call does not include a ref in the path — it relies on Bitbucket's default branch
When ref IS provided, it is used in the path as before
The input schema description for ref says 'defaults to the repository default branch' (not 'defaults to main')
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/bitbucket/src/tools/get-file-content.ts (remove hardcoded 'main')

Context:
Line 26:
```typescript
const ref = params.ref ?? 'main';
const content = await apiRaw(`/repositories/${params.workspace}/${params.repo_slug}/src/${ref}/${params.path}`);
```

Bitbucket's Source API at `/repositories/{workspace}/{repo_slug}/src/{revision}/{path}` uses the repo's default branch when the revision segment is omitted or set to the empty string. However, the path structure requires a revision segment.

The fix depends on the Bitbucket API behavior:
- Option A: If omitting the revision segment works (e.g., `/src/${params.path}`), use that when ref is undefined
- Option B: Use `HEAD` as the revision (Bitbucket supports this to mean the default branch)
- Option C: Make a separate API call to get the repo's default branch when ref is not provided

Option B is simplest:
```typescript
const ref = params.ref ?? 'HEAD';
```

Check the Bitbucket API docs or test to confirm `HEAD` works as a revision alias.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the apiRaw function
- Keep the params.ref as optional in the input schema
